roadmap.milestone_count_rationale: 5 milestones: Aarav is a senior professional with 6 years of experience, a clear target role, and high urgency. 5 identity steps provide a balanced progression from a strong backend engineer to a senior architect, covering significant skill gaps in system design, distributed systems, and cloud architecture, while respecting the available weekly hours for deep learning.

vision_profile.current_state: A Backend Engineer proficient in Java, Spring Boot, REST, PostgreSQL, and Redis, primarily executing assigned tasks.
vision_profile.main_blocker: Aarav's biggest obstacle is a perceived lack of depth in system design, distributed systems, and cloud architecture, which he fears will hinder his advancement to a senior role.
vision_profile.top_motivation: He is highly motivated to own larger engineering decisions and move beyond merely executing tickets, seeking to lead architectural initiatives.

milestones.label: M01
milestones.o: Learner can analyze system requirements, propose high-level designs, and articulate trade-offs for common backend services, moving beyond mere implementation.
milestones.module_count_rationale: 3 modules are chosen to cover the essential breadth for foundational system architecture: core principles, practical backend service patterns, and crucial design communication skills.
milestones.identity_statement: You are transitioning from a skilled executor to a thoughtful architect, capable of shaping the 'what' and 'why' of system design.

project.title: Design & Prototype a Scalable API Gateway
project.description: Design and prototype a robust API Gateway for a microservices ecosystem, focusing on routing, authentication, and rate limiting. You will make architectural decisions and implement a basic proof-of-concept.
project.deliverable: High-level design document, architectural diagrams, and a working prototype demonstrating core gateway functionalities.
seeded_errors: The initial rate-limiting algorithm chosen by the AI is inefficient for high-concurrency scenarios, leading to false positives and degraded performance.

modules.title: Core System Design Principles
modules.skill_count_rationale: 4 skills are necessary to establish a strong theoretical foundation in system design, covering the fundamental non-functional requirements.

skills.title: Scalability Concepts & Patterns
skills.why_this_skill: Understanding how to scale systems horizontally and vertically is crucial for designing robust backend services that can handle increasing load.

scenario.title: Scaling a Monolithic E-commerce Backend

skills.title: High Availability & Reliability
skills.why_this_skill: Designing for fault tolerance and continuous operation is critical for senior roles, ensuring systems remain accessible and functional even during failures.

scenario.title: Designing for 99.99% Uptime

skills.title: Performance Optimization Techniques
skills.why_this_skill: Understanding how to identify and resolve performance bottlenecks is a core responsibility of a senior backend engineer, impacting user experience and operational costs.

scenario.title: Debugging a Slow API Endpoint

skills.title: System Design Trade-off Analysis
skills.why_this_skill: A senior engineer must evaluate competing design choices based on cost, complexity, performance, and maintainability, making informed decisions for the business.

scenario.title: Choosing a Database for a New Service

modules.title: Backend Service Architecture
modules.skill_count_rationale: 4 skills are essential to apply foundational principles to practical backend service design, leveraging existing knowledge while introducing advanced patterns.

skills.title: Advanced API Design Principles
skills.why_this_skill: Building on existing REST API knowledge, this skill focuses on designing robust, evolvable, and user-friendly APIs, critical for microservices and external integrations.

scenario.title: Refactoring a Legacy API for Microservices

skills.title: Microservices Architecture Patterns
skills.why_this_skill: Moving towards senior roles often involves working with or designing microservices. Understanding their patterns, benefits, and challenges is key.

scenario.title: Designing a New Microservice Boundary

skills.title: Database Selection & Scaling Strategies
skills.why_this_skill: Beyond knowing PostgreSQL, a senior engineer must choose the right database for the job and design its scaling strategy, considering various data models and consistency requirements.

scenario.title: Migrating a Monolith Database to Microservices

skills.title: Advanced Caching Strategies
skills.why_this_skill: Leveraging Redis, this skill deepens understanding of various caching patterns, invalidation strategies, and their impact on performance and data consistency in distributed systems.

scenario.title: Diagnosing a Latency Spike in a Microservice API

science.desc: A critical microservice API is experiencing intermittent latency spikes under moderate load. Investigate the caching layer and database interactions to identify the root cause and propose a solution.

modules.title: Technical Design Communication
modules.skill_count_rationale: 4 skills are crucial for a senior engineer to effectively document, present, and iterate on technical designs, addressing the 'technical design documentation' gap.

skills.title: Technical Design Document Creation
skills.why_this_skill: Mastering the creation of clear, concise, and comprehensive design documents is essential for communicating complex architectural decisions and ensuring alignment across teams.

scenario.title: Reviewing a Flawed Design Document

skills.title: UML & Architecture Diagramming
skills.why_this_skill: Visual communication of complex systems is paramount. This skill teaches standard diagramming techniques to convey architectural intent clearly.

scenario.title: Creating Diagrams for a New Feature

skills.title: Presenting Technical Designs & Proposals
skills.why_this_skill: A senior engineer must effectively articulate and defend design choices to peers, stakeholders, and leadership, influencing technical direction.

scenario.title: Leading a Design Review for a Critical Feature

skills.title: Design Feedback Integration & Iteration
skills.why_this_skill: Architectural design is an iterative process. Incorporating feedback constructively and evolving designs is a hallmark of senior engineering leadership.

scenario.title: Resolving Conflicting Design Feedback

science.desc: Design a URL Shortening Service like TinyURL. Discuss its core components, data model, scalability challenges, and how you would handle collisions and custom URLs.

milestones.label: M02
milestones.o: Learner can design, implement, and troubleshoot robust distributed systems, effectively utilizing messaging queues and ensuring data consistency.
milestones.module_count_rationale: 3 modules are dedicated to mastering distributed systems: foundational concepts, event-driven architectures (including Kafka), and critical aspects of consistency and concurrency.
milestones.identity_statement: You are evolving into an expert in distributed systems, capable of building resilient and scalable services that communicate seamlessly.

project.title: Build an Event-Driven Order Processing System
project.description: Design and implement an event-driven order processing system using Kafka for asynchronous communication between services (e.g., Order Service, Inventory Service, Payment Service). Focus on message reliability and idempotency.
project.deliverable: Architectural diagrams, Kafka topic designs, and a working multi-service application demonstrating order creation, inventory update, and payment processing via events.
seeded_errors: The initial Kafka consumer implementation does not handle duplicate messages, leading to double-processing of orders under specific failure scenarios.

modules.title: Distributed System Fundamentals
modules.skill_count_rationale: 4 skills provide the essential theoretical and practical understanding of distributed system challenges and solutions.

skills.title: Challenges in Distributed Systems
skills.why_this_skill: Understanding the inherent complexities like network partitions, clock skew, and partial failures is foundational for designing reliable distributed applications.

scenario.title: Diagnosing a Network Partition Issue

skills.title: Remote Procedure Calls (RPC)
skills.why_this_skill: Understanding RPC frameworks like gRPC is vital for high-performance, language-agnostic service communication in distributed environments.

scenario.title: Integrating a New Service with gRPC

skills.title: Distributed Transactions & Sagas
skills.why_this_skill: Managing data consistency across multiple services in a distributed environment is complex. This skill covers patterns to ensure atomicity and reliability.

scenario.title: Designing a Payment Processing Workflow

skills.title: Consensus Algorithms (e.g., Raft)
skills.why_this_skill: Understanding how distributed systems achieve agreement on a single data value is crucial for building highly consistent and fault-tolerant services.

scenario.title: Debugging a Distributed Lock Service

modules.title: Messaging & Event-Driven Architectures
modules.skill_count_rationale: 4 skills are necessary to deeply understand and implement event-driven patterns, with a specific focus on Kafka architecture to address the user's skill gap.

skills.title: Message Queues & Brokers
skills.why_this_skill: Understanding the role of message queues is fundamental for asynchronous communication, decoupling services, and building scalable, fault-tolerant systems.

scenario.title: Choosing a Message Broker for a New Service

skills.title: Kafka Architecture & Operations
skills.why_this_skill: Directly addresses a user skill gap. Mastering Kafka's architecture is crucial for building high-throughput, fault-tolerant data pipelines and event-driven microservices.

scenario.title: Designing a Kafka Topic for High Throughput

skills.title: Event Sourcing & CQRS Patterns
skills.why_this_skill: These advanced patterns are crucial for complex distributed systems, enabling better scalability, auditability, and flexibility in data models.

scenario.title: Designing an Audit Log with Event Sourcing

skills.title: Message Idempotency & Delivery Guarantees
skills.why_this_skill: Ensuring messages are processed exactly once, even in the face of failures, is critical for data integrity in event-driven systems.

scenario.title: Debugging Duplicate Order Processing

science.desc: A critical Kafka consumer is falling behind, leading to stale data in downstream services. Analyze consumer group metrics, partition assignments, and message processing logic to identify the bottleneck and propose a scaling strategy.

modules.title: Consistency & Concurrency
modules.skill_count_rationale: 4 skills are vital for managing data integrity and concurrent access in highly distributed and multi-threaded environments.

skills.title: Concurrency Primitives in Java
skills.why_this_skill: Building on Java knowledge, this skill deepens understanding of how to manage concurrent access to shared resources, preventing race conditions and deadlocks.

scenario.title: Debugging a Concurrency Bug in a Shared Cache

skills.title: Distributed Locks & Coordination
skills.why_this_skill: Ensuring exclusive access to resources across multiple service instances is a common challenge in distributed systems, requiring specialized coordination mechanisms.

scenario.title: Preventing Double-Booking in a Reservation System

skills.title: Data Consistency Models
skills.why_this_skill: Understanding different consistency models (e.g., strong, eventual) is crucial for making informed trade-offs in distributed database and caching designs.

scenario.title: Designing a Globally Distributed User Profile Service

skills.title: Designing Idempotent Operations
skills.why_this_skill: Crucial for fault-tolerant systems, idempotent operations ensure that repeated requests produce the same result, preventing unintended side effects.

scenario.title: Implementing Idempotency for a Payment Gateway

science.desc: Explain the CAP Theorem and its implications for distributed system design. Provide examples of systems that prioritize consistency, availability, and partition tolerance, respectively.

milestones.label: M03
milestones.o: Learner can architect, deploy, and manage scalable and resilient backend applications using AWS cloud services and modern deployment patterns.
milestones.module_count_rationale: 3 modules are structured to provide comprehensive AWS cloud architecture skills: core services, serverless/containerized deployment patterns, and cloud-native data management.
milestones.identity_statement: You are now a cloud-native expert, leveraging AWS to build and deploy highly scalable and resilient backend systems.

project.title: Deploy a Serverless Microservice on AWS
project.description: Architect and deploy a serverless microservice on AWS using Lambda, API Gateway, and DynamoDB. Implement CI/CD for automated deployments and ensure proper monitoring and logging.
project.deliverable: CloudFormation/Terraform templates, deployed and functional serverless API, and a monitoring dashboard showing key metrics.
seeded_errors: The initial Lambda function's IAM role has overly permissive access, violating the principle of least privilege and posing a security risk.

modules.title: AWS Core Services for Backend
modules.skill_count_rationale: 4 skills cover the fundamental AWS services essential for any backend architecture, providing a solid base for cloud deployments.

skills.title: AWS EC2 & VPC Fundamentals
skills.why_this_skill: Understanding virtual machines and networking in AWS is foundational for deploying and securing any cloud-based backend infrastructure.

scenario.title: Designing a Secure VPC for a Multi-Tier Application

skills.title: AWS Load Balancing & Auto Scaling
skills.why_this_skill: Ensuring high availability and elasticity for backend services in the cloud requires mastery of AWS's load balancing and auto-scaling capabilities.

scenario.title: Configuring Auto Scaling for a Web Application

skills.title: AWS IAM & Security Best Practices
skills.why_this_skill: Security is paramount in the cloud. Mastering IAM is critical for managing access control and adhering to the principle of least privilege.

scenario.title: Auditing IAM Policies for Compliance

skills.title: Infrastructure as Code (CloudFormation/Terraform)
skills.why_this_skill: Automating infrastructure provisioning is a core DevOps practice. Mastering IaC tools is essential for repeatable, consistent, and scalable cloud deployments.

scenario.title: Deploying a Multi-Tier Application with Terraform

modules.title: Serverless & Containerized Deployments
modules.skill_count_rationale: 4 skills directly address the user's 'AWS deployment patterns' gap, focusing on modern, scalable, and cost-effective cloud deployment strategies.

skills.title: AWS Lambda & API Gateway
skills.why_this_skill: Mastering serverless functions and API management is crucial for building highly scalable, cost-effective, and maintenance-free backend services.

scenario.title: Optimizing a Lambda Function for Performance

skills.title: AWS ECS & Fargate for Containers
skills.why_this_skill: Containerization is a standard for microservices. ECS and Fargate provide managed solutions for deploying and scaling containerized applications without managing underlying servers.

scenario.title: Migrating a Spring Boot App to ECS Fargate

skills.title: AWS EKS & Kubernetes Basics
skills.why_this_skill: For complex, large-scale container orchestration, Kubernetes (and EKS on AWS) is the industry standard. This provides essential knowledge for advanced deployments.

scenario.title: Troubleshooting a Failed EKS Deployment

skills.title: CI/CD with AWS DevOps Tools
skills.why_this_skill: Automating the software delivery pipeline is essential for rapid, reliable deployments in a cloud-native environment, improving developer velocity and system stability.

scenario.title: Setting up a CI/CD Pipeline for a Microservice

science.desc: A newly deployed serverless API on AWS Lambda is experiencing unexpected cold starts and high latency. Analyze CloudWatch logs and X-Ray traces to identify the cause and implement optimizations.

modules.title: Cloud Data Management
modules.skill_count_rationale: 4 skills focus on managing data effectively in the cloud, covering both relational and NoSQL options, and ensuring data security and backup.

skills.title: AWS RDS & Aurora for Relational Databases
skills.why_this_skill: Managing relational databases in the cloud requires understanding managed services like RDS and Aurora for scalability, high availability, and operational ease.

scenario.title: Scaling a PostgreSQL Database with RDS Aurora

skills.title: AWS DynamoDB & NoSQL Design
skills.why_this_skill: For high-performance, flexible, and scalable NoSQL solutions, DynamoDB is a key AWS service. Understanding its design patterns is essential for modern cloud applications.

scenario.title: Modeling a User Profile for DynamoDB

skills.title: AWS S3 & Storage Solutions
skills.why_this_skill: S3 is the backbone of cloud storage. Understanding its capabilities, cost models, and integration patterns is essential for managing static assets, backups, and data lakes.

scenario.title: Designing a Cost-Effective Backup Strategy with S3

skills.title: AWS Data Security & Backup Strategies
skills.why_this_skill: Ensuring data security, compliance, and robust backup/recovery plans are critical responsibilities for any senior cloud architect.

scenario.title: Responding to a Data Breach Incident in AWS

science.desc: You need to host a highly available and scalable e-commerce application on AWS. Describe your architectural choices for compute, database, and storage, justifying your decisions based on cost, performance, and reliability.

milestones.label: M04
milestones.o: Learner can lead efforts to build, monitor, and maintain highly available, observable, and secure distributed systems, effectively communicating complex technical designs.
milestones.module_count_rationale: 3 modules are designed to cultivate leadership in system resilience: dedicated focus on reliability engineering, comprehensive observability, and advanced security practices.
milestones.identity_statement: You are now a leader in system resilience, ensuring the stability, performance, and security of complex distributed architectures.

project.title: Implement Observability for a Distributed System
project.description: Integrate comprehensive logging, metrics, and tracing into an existing microservices application. Design and implement a dashboard to monitor key performance indicators and identify anomalies.
project.deliverable: Instrumented microservices, configured logging and monitoring tools (e.g., Prometheus, Grafana, Jaeger), and a detailed report on system health and performance.
seeded_errors: The initial logging configuration produces excessive, unparsed log data, making it difficult to extract meaningful insights and incurring high storage costs.

modules.title: Reliability Engineering
modules.skill_count_rationale: 4 skills are critical for proactively designing and managing systems for maximum uptime and fault tolerance, a core aspect of senior leadership.

skills.title: Site Reliability Engineering (SRE) Principles
skills.why_this_skill: Adopting SRE principles is key for senior engineers to balance development velocity with operational stability, ensuring long-term system health.

scenario.title: Defining SLOs for a Critical Microservice

skills.title: Chaos Engineering & Resilience Testing
skills.why_this_skill: Proactively identifying weaknesses by intentionally injecting failures is a hallmark of highly resilient systems, preparing for real-world outages.

scenario.title: Conducting a Chaos Experiment on a Microservice

skills.title: Disaster Recovery & Business Continuity
skills.why_this_skill: Planning for catastrophic failures and ensuring rapid recovery is a critical responsibility for senior engineers, minimizing downtime and data loss.

scenario.title: Designing a Disaster Recovery Plan for a Critical Service

skills.title: Incident Management & Post-Mortems
skills.why_this_skill: Effectively responding to, resolving, and learning from production incidents is a key leadership skill, driving continuous improvement in system reliability.

scenario.title: Leading a Post-Mortem for a Major Outage

modules.title: Observability & Monitoring
modules.skill_count_rationale: 4 skills are essential for gaining deep insights into system behavior, enabling proactive issue detection and rapid debugging in complex distributed environments.

skills.title: Distributed Logging & Aggregation
skills.why_this_skill: Centralized logging is crucial for debugging and auditing distributed systems. This skill covers best practices for log collection, parsing, and analysis.

scenario.title: Debugging a Production Issue Using Log Aggregation

skills.title: Metrics, Monitoring & Alerting
skills.why_this_skill: Defining and collecting key metrics, setting up effective monitoring, and configuring actionable alerts are vital for maintaining system health and performance.

scenario.title: Configuring Alerts for Critical Service Health

skills.title: Distributed Tracing & Profiling
skills.why_this_skill: Tracing requests across multiple services is indispensable for understanding performance bottlenecks and failure points in complex distributed architectures.

scenario.title: Identifying Latency Hotspots with Distributed Tracing

skills.title: Synthetic & Real User Monitoring (RUM)
skills.why_this_skill: Proactive monitoring of user experience and system availability from an external perspective is crucial for understanding real-world impact and performance.

scenario.title: Investigating a Reported User Experience Degradation

science.desc: A critical production service is experiencing intermittent 5xx errors, but logs and metrics show no obvious issues. Utilize distributed tracing to pinpoint the exact service and code path causing the failures.

modules.title: Security in Distributed Systems
modules.skill_count_rationale: 4 skills are essential for designing and implementing secure distributed systems, covering authentication, authorization, data protection, and vulnerability management.

skills.title: Authentication & Authorization
skills.why_this_skill: Implementing robust identity and access management is fundamental for securing distributed services and protecting sensitive data.

scenario.title: Designing an Authentication Flow for a New Microservice

skills.title: Data Encryption (In-transit & At-rest)
skills.why_this_skill: Protecting sensitive data through encryption is a fundamental security requirement. This skill covers best practices for both data in motion and data at rest.

scenario.title: Implementing End-to-End Encryption for a Data Pipeline

skills.title: API Security & Threat Modeling
skills.why_this_skill: Securing APIs from common vulnerabilities and proactively identifying potential threats are critical for protecting backend services from attacks.

scenario.title: Conducting a Threat Model for a New Payment API

skills.title: Vulnerability Management & Patching
skills.why_this_skill: Proactive identification and remediation of security vulnerabilities are essential for maintaining a secure software supply chain and protecting production systems.

scenario.title: Responding to a Critical CVE in a Production Dependency

science.desc: You are tasked with securing a new microservices platform. Outline your strategy for authentication, authorization, data protection, and vulnerability management across the entire system.

milestones.label: M05
milestones.o: Learner can lead large-scale architectural initiatives, drive strategic technical decisions, and mentor other engineers, demonstrating mastery in complex system design and evolution.
milestones.module_count_rationale: 3 modules are designed to elevate the learner to a principal architect level, focusing on advanced design patterns, architectural evolution, and critical technical leadership skills.
milestones.identity_statement: You are now a Principal Backend Architect, a visionary leader shaping the future of complex systems and mentoring the next generation of engineers.

project.title: Architect a Global Real-time Data Platform
project.description: Design a highly scalable, fault-tolerant, and globally distributed real-time data platform capable of ingesting, processing, and serving billions of events per day. Focus on architectural trade-offs, data consistency, and operational complexity.
project.deliverable: Comprehensive architectural proposal, detailed design document with component choices, data flow diagrams, and a presentation to a mock architecture review board.
seeded_errors: The proposed data partitioning strategy for global distribution introduces significant cross-region latency for certain query patterns, which the learner must identify and optimize.

modules.title: Advanced System Design Patterns
modules.skill_count_rationale: 4 skills delve into sophisticated design patterns, enabling the architect to tackle highly complex and large-scale system challenges.

skills.title: Designing for Extreme Scale & Performance
skills.why_this_skill: Architecting systems for billions of users or transactions requires specialized knowledge in high-performance computing, data partitioning, and distributed caching.

scenario.title: Scaling a Global Social Media Feed

skills.title: Multi-Region & Global Architectures
skills.why_this_skill: Designing systems that span multiple geographical regions is critical for global reach, low latency, and enhanced disaster recovery capabilities.

scenario.title: Architecting a Globally Distributed Database

skills.title: Real-time Streaming Architectures
skills.why_this_skill: Designing systems that process and react to data in real-time is increasingly important for analytics, fraud detection, and interactive applications.

scenario.title: Designing a Real-time Fraud Detection System

skills.title: Advanced API Gateway & Service Mesh
skills.why_this_skill: For complex microservices environments, advanced API gateways and service meshes provide critical capabilities for traffic management, security, and observability.

scenario.title: Implementing a Service Mesh for a Microservices Platform

modules.title: Architectural Evolution & Refactoring
modules.skill_count_rationale: 4 skills focus on the strategic aspects of evolving and maintaining large-scale systems, including technical debt management and future-proofing architectures.

skills.title: Monolith to Microservices Migration Strategies
skills.why_this_skill: Leading large-scale refactoring efforts is a common task for principal architects. This covers patterns and challenges in migrating legacy systems.

scenario.title: Planning a Monolith Decomposition Project

skills.title: Technical Debt Management
skills.why_this_skill: Strategically managing technical debt is crucial for long-term system health and developer productivity, a key responsibility of a principal architect.

scenario.title: Proposing a Technical Debt Reduction Initiative

skills.title: Architecture Governance & Standards
skills.why_this_skill: Establishing and enforcing architectural standards ensures consistency, maintainability, and security across an organization's engineering efforts.

scenario.title: Implementing a New Architectural Review Process

skills.title: Future-Proofing Architectures
skills.why_this_skill: Designing systems that can adapt to future requirements, technologies, and business changes is a strategic skill for principal architects.

scenario.title: Evaluating a New Technology for Strategic Adoption

science.desc: Your company is acquiring a new business with a legacy monolithic system. Develop a strategic plan for integrating its functionalities into your existing microservices platform, considering data migration, service decomposition, and minimal business disruption.

modules.title: Technical Leadership & Mentorship
modules.skill_count_rationale: 4 skills are dedicated to developing the soft and hard leadership capabilities expected of a principal architect, focusing on influence, mentorship, and strategic impact.

skills.title: Influencing Technical Direction & Strategy
skills.why_this_skill: A principal architect drives technical strategy. This skill focuses on building consensus, articulating vision, and influencing key decisions across the organization.

scenario.title: Presenting a Strategic Architectural Roadmap

skills.title: Mentorship & Technical Coaching
skills.why_this_skill: A principal architect is a force multiplier, responsible for growing the technical capabilities of their team and fostering a culture of excellence.

scenario.title: Mentoring a Junior Engineer on a Complex Design Task

skills.title: Cross-Functional Collaboration & Alignment
skills.why_this_skill: Driving successful outcomes in complex organizations requires strong collaboration with product, operations, and other engineering teams.
lessons: Communicating Technical Constraints to Non-Technical Stakeholders

scenario.title: Resolving a Conflict Between Engineering and Product Teams

skills.title: Strategic Technical Decision Making
skills.why_this_skill: At the highest level, architects make decisions that impact the entire organization, balancing short-term needs with long-term strategic goals.

scenario.title: Evaluating a Build vs. Buy Decision for a Core System Component

science.desc: As a Principal Backend Architect, how would you approach a situation where a critical business initiative requires a significant architectural change that introduces substantial technical debt? How would you balance short-term delivery with long-term system health?
